events.text: {"decision":"fail","risk":"medium","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant","worn","degraded"],"percent_complete":80,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.8,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Skirtboard gap wear observed on conveyor system at rough-complete stage, allowing material spillage and creating pinch point exposure","Worn skirtboard sealing/gap exceeds manufacturer tolerances, reducing containment and increasing entanglement hazard"],"actions":["Replace or adjust skirtboard seals to restore designed gap clearance","Verify guard coverage at skirtboard per OSHA 1910.212 before return to service","Re-inspect after correction at quality gate"],"rationale":"At rough-complete stage, skirtboard gap wear indicates the conveyor is installed but defective and non-compliant with CEMA design/maintenance guidance and OSHA 1910.212 guarding requirements; worn gaps allow spillage and increase pinch/entanglement hazards, so the work fails the quality gate until corrected.","references":["CEMA","OSHA 1910.212"]}
